rulings.Case Title: NOELLE BONNER VS. ALEX KWAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Petition To Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, line 2. RESPONDENTS ALEX KWAK, APPLEPALM ENTERPRISES, INC'S Petition To Correct Arbitration Award. Petitioners Alex Kwak and Applepalm Enterprises, Inc.'s petition to correct arbitration award is treated as a motion to correct a clerical mistake in the court's judgment, and is denied as to the request to correct the amount of the award and is granted as to the incorrect naming of Alex Kwak as a judgment debtor. The court has the inherent power under Code of Civil Procedure Section 473(d) to correct clerical mistakes in its judgment. The motion to correct the arbitration award as to the sum awarded is both untimely and unfounded. Because the court intended to confirm the arbitration award, to extent that the judgment failed to conform to the arbitration award, the court retains inherent power to correct the judgment nunc pro tunc and excise from the judgment "Alex Kwak" as a person against whom recovery is adjudged. (See Ames v. Paley (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 668, 674 ["because the trial court intended to enter judgment pursuant to the settlement agreement, to the extent the judgment failed to conform to the terms of the settlement agreement, the trial court retained the inherent power to correct the judgment nunc pro tunc some three months after entry of the judgment."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for petitioners is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. VS. LUIS ZAMBRANO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ALIGN TECHNOLOGY, INC. Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order Judge Pro Tem Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Judge Pro Tem. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Judge Pro Tem will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED, no opposition filed. Moving party to submit a revised proposed order without the section entitled "Notice to Judgment Debtor" attached. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN CLOPTON VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Discovery Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, line 5. DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, MICHAEL PICKER, LIANE RANDOLPH'S Motion To Stay Discovery Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order. The court adopts the report and recommendation of the judge pro tem. Defendants California Public Utilities Commission, Michael Picker, and Liane M. Randolph's ill-conceived motion to stay discovery, or in the alternative, for a protective order is denied. Defendants contend that certain of Plaintiff's claims are barred by collateral estoppel or by her failure to exhaust judicial remedies because she did not file a petition for writ of mandate challenging the State Personnel Board's decision upholding her termination. However, the Court will not adjudicate the merits of those contested defenses under the guise of a stay of discovery or a protective order. As Plaintiff correctly argues, "a motion to stay discovery or for a protective order is the wrong motion to argue exhaustion and estoppel." If Defendants wish to raise those issues, they may do so by filing a motion for summary judgment/adjudication as to the purportedly barred claims. Far from being "directly on point," none of the authorities Defendants rely upon supports the relief sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLOTTE KERNAN ET AL VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, line 7. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the complaint is sustained without leave to amend. It is undisputed that the filing requirements of the Government Claims Act apply to plaintiffs' negligence cause of action against the City. (Gov't Code §§ 905, 905.2, 945.4.) The City's records from the Claims Division show that no claim was filed. The court takes judicial notice of the Rothschild declaration pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(c) and Gong v. City of Rosemead (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 363, 376. Plaintiffs allege that they sent a letter with their claim to Susan Ehrlich, CEO of the Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital, and a number of physicians. However, the Government Claims Act requires that a claim be actually received by "the clerk, secretary, auditor, or board" of the local public entity, and the San Francisco Administrative Code provides that the claim must be delivered to the Clerk of the Board of Supervisors or Controller of the City and County. (Gov't Code § 915(e)(1); SF Admin. Code § 10.20-4.) "The goals of the claims statutes are to provide entities with sufficient information to investigate and appropriately resolve claims and to plan for potential liabilities. These goals are satisfied when an adequate claim is actually received by an appropriate entity representative. The Legislature retains authority to determine which representatives are appropriate." (DiCampli-Mintz v. County of Santa Clara (2012) 55 Cal.4th 983, 994.) Thus, unless Plaintiffs can allege in good faith that their claim was actually received by one of the statutorily designated recipients within the time prescribed for presentation, the demurrer will be sustained without leave to amend. (See id. at 992-993 [holding that a misdirected claim fails to comply with the statute, and will satisfy the presentation requirement only if it is actually received by a statutorily designated recipient].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JANE ALEXIADIS ET AL VS. ALICIA M. GAMEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, line 11. DEFENDANT ALICIA GAMEZ'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Alicia M. Gámez's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is granted. The alternative motion to dismiss pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 389 (b) is moot in light of the court's ruling granting defendant's motion to quash service of summons. According to Ms. Gomez's declaration, she is providing legal services as a natural person and "the Law Office of Alicia Gámez" is a fictitious name under which she is doing business but is not a separate legal entity. (Gámez Decl. 3(c)(d); see Providence Washington Ins. Co. v. Valley Forge Ins. Co. (1996) 42 Cal.App.4th 1194, 1200 ["Doing business under another name does not create an entity distinct from the person operating the business. The business name is a fiction, and so too is any implication that the business is a legal entity separate from its owner."]; Ball v. Steadfast-BLK (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 694, 701. Failure to attempt personal service on an individual defendant before effecting substitute service falls short of substantial compliance with the service statutes. (Code Civ. Proc. § 415.20(b); Local Rule 6.6(c); see Evartt v. Superior Court (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 795, 802 ["substitute abode service on petitioner was ineffective and void because real party failed to comply with the mandatory prerequisite to such service by exercising reasonable diligence to effect personal service."].) Furthermore, under Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 415, leaving the complaint with defendant's legal assistant, who was not authorized to accept service on behalf of defendant, does not constitute substantial compliance with the statute. (Gámez Decl. 3(e)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: 631 FOLSOM STREET OWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. FOLSOM VENTURES LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on the Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 5, 2019, line 12. DEFENDANTS FOLSOM VENTURES, LLC, LENNAR HOMES OF CALIFORNIA, INC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendants Folsom Venture, LLC and Lennar Homes of California, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is overruled. McMillin Albany LLC v. Superior Court (2018) 4 Cal.5th 241 does not bar the breach of express warranty, breach of implied warranty, and violation of CC&Rs causes of action. All of those claims sound in contract and therefore they are viable. (See Civ. Code § 943(a) ["[T]his title does not apply to any action by a claimant to enforce a contract¿"]; McMillan Albany LLC, 4 Cal.5th at 249 ["the statute here leaves the common law undisturbed in some areas, expressly preserving actions for breach of contract, fraud, and personal injury. (§ 943, subd. (a).)."]; id. at 259 ["Nor does our holding embrace claims such as those for breach of contract¿"].) The court concludes that the breach of implied warranty claim sounds in contract in this case. "A warranty is a contractual term concerning some aspect of the sale¿" (Windham v. Superior Court (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th1162, 1168; see also Freas v. BMW of North America, LLC (2018) 320 F.Supp.3d 1126, 1132 ["Under California law, a claim for breach of express or implied warranties 'necessarily sounds in contract.'"]; Allstate Ins. Co. v. Loo (1996) 46 Cal.App.4th 1794, 1798 ["Allstate did assert a contractual cause of action: breach of the implied warranty of habitability."].) The court further notes that plaintiff is seeking purely economic and property damages. "The precise nature of the claim is ordinarily identified by examining the damages alleged: when the damages are purely economic, the claim sounds in contract, but a ... claim alleging damages for death or personal injury sounds in tort." (Rodriguez v. Christus Spohn Health System Corp. (5th Cir. 2010) 628 F.3d 731, 736.) Lennar Homes of California, Inc. contends that it lacks privity with plaintiff on the CC&Rs. Paragraph 12 of the complaint alleges that Folsom Ventures, LLC and Lennar are alter egos and there is a factual issue regarding who is bound by the CC&Rs. Defendants argue that the breach of express warranty claim does not highlight any specific warranty provision. Plaintiffs sufficiently plead the terms of the contracts according to their legal intendment and effect. To plead a contract claim according to its legal effect, the plaintiff should allege the making and then proceed to allege the substance of its relevant terms. (See McKell v. Washington Mut. Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1489; Pneucrete Corp. v. United States Fidelity & Guaranty Co. (1935) 7 Cal.App.2d 733, 741-742.) Plaintiffs have done so in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
